narrative: OMX30 enters Wednesday session with solid European tailwinds and a constructive technical setup, sitting 9.9% above the 200-day MA in confirmed bull market regime. Stochastic overbought (K=84.4, D=86.5) signals potential mean reversion, though MACD remains above signal and ADX weak suggests low conviction directionally—expect range-bound consolidation rather than breakout. Currency weakness (SEK softening vs EUR and USD) is a net positive for Volvo, Atlas Copco, and Ericsson, offsetting the NASDAQ lag. Energy selloff (Brent -6.26%) removes near-term cost inflation fears, benefiting consumer spending and industrial margins. Key risk: Stochastic mean reversion if OMX30 breaks below 50-MA support at ~3100; watch for any trade policy escalation tied to Trump headlines.
morningBrief: OMX30 should open with +0.3% to +0.5% bias on European strength and soft oil. Bull structure intact (9.9% above 200-MA, MACD positive), but Stochastic deeply overbought (K=84, D=86) flags consolidation risk—treat 3180 (50-MA) as first resistance and 3085 (lower Bollinger) as critical support. Sectors: Industrials and exporters get a tailwind from SEK weakness; banks neutral to positive on yield stability; mining (Boliden) supported by copper/gold bid. Watch H&M carefully—weak EUR/SEK raises sourcing headwinds despite oil relief. ADX at 4.9 (weak) argues for caution on magnitude; don't chase breakouts. Key event: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—any surprise could trigger volatility given Fed narrative sensitivity. Trade tight ranges and favor defensive/high-quality longs.
drivers: European equity strength (DAX +0.59%, CAC +0.55%) with positive correlation signal
OMX30 well above 200-MA (+9.9%) in established bull market structure
Copper +0.41% and gold +0.60% supporting mining/materials exposure
Weak SEK (USD/SEK -0.36%, EUR/SEK -0.30%) favoring major exporters
Oil -6.26% reduces input cost headwinds for industrials and consumer sectors

sectorImpacts.reason: Weak SEK boosts export competitiveness; Brent -6.26% reduces input cost pressure on machinery/engineering.
US 10Y yield at 4.493% stable (-1.43%); Swedish rates likely unchanged. NIM support flat but baseline solid.
Gold +0.60% and copper +0.41% deliver direct revenue uplift; Boliden primary beneficiary.
NASDAQ +1.19% overnight offsets valuation concerns; weak SEK aids Ericsson export pricing. Maintain neutral bias—ADX weak.
Oil relief supports consumer spending, but weak EUR/SEK lifts H&M sourcing costs (net negative). Gaming sentiment neutral.
Yields stable; no material refinancing pressure. Hold neutral positioning.
Defensive positioning supported by VIX 16.97, but no material catalyst. Maintain hold.

watchList.reason: Copper +0.41% and gold +0.60% are direct revenue tailwinds; strong commodity support overnight.
SEK weakness (USD/SEK -0.36%, EUR/SEK -0.30%) improves export pricing; industrials sector tailwind on lower oil.
Weak EUR/SEK (+0.09%) raises sourcing costs despite oil relief. Monitor near-term margin pressure.
NASDAQ +1.19% overnight offsets valuation compression; SEK weakness adds support. Range-trade setup.
Stable yields keep NIM outlook intact; no material catalyst expected pre-Jobless Claims.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases. US Jobless Claims (initial) at 14:30 CET—watch for surprise upside/downside; consensus ~215K. Any significant miss could ripple through SEK and equity futures.
topRisk: Stochastic mean reversion (K=84.4, D=86.5 overbought) could trigger -0.5% pullback to 3100 if support breaks. Weak ADX (4.9) means low conviction on direction—avoid size above 3180. Trade war escalation via Trump headlines remains tail risk (BP boardroom scandal, Board of Peace fund empty—sentiment risk, not immediate).
technicalSummary: Stochastic deeply overbought (K=84.4, D=86.5)—mean reversion risk
52-week: 94% into range, -1.6% from high (limited upside room)
